text: This paper presents a new interpretative key for assessing the competitiveness of a tourism destination through the means of its tangible and intangible cultural assets and local creative industries. Through a review of the literature concerning destination management, from an Italian and international perspective, the authors analyze the relations between cultural heritage and “content industries” such as design, architecture and digital media and the ways in which these relationships can support the economic development of a territory. Starting from a context in which the conservation of cultural heritage was often the prevalent focus, the paper passes into a perspective on “creative tourism” in which the territory works as an human capital and as a hub for social innovation. Through the analysis of the multipliers and of the economic impact on the context in which they operate, based on international and national case studies in Europe and Italy, the paper finally illustrates a three-axis model (territorial attractions, tourism infrastructure and the system of culture and creativity) and the potentialities and challenges it presents for managers and policymakers that have to promote the sustainable well-being of local communities while at the same time ensuring the competitiveness of each destination in the global market.
explanation: The passage uses very polished, high-level academic phrasing with generic content and no distinctive personal voice, which is common in AI-generated text. This yields a moderate-to-high probability that AI assisted the writing.
